title: Review & Setlist: ‘We are foreign imports, so we have paid tariffs.’
author: Marc Hirsh
bullets: The Hives performed at Boston's House of Blues with The Chats as the opening act.
Frontman Howlin’ Pelle Almqvist engaged the crowd with charismatic leadership and energetic performances.
The band's setlist included tracks from their latest album and iconic hits like 'Hate To Say I Told You So.'

title: Cancer couldn’t stop her. Now she’s running the Boston Marathon again.
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Stephanie McHugh, 42, from Braintree, is running her third Boston Marathon in 2026 for the Patriots Charitable Foundation.
McHugh returned to running after overcoming Stage 3 breast cancer, which she was diagnosed with two months after turning 40.
Inspired by friend Susan Hurley, McHugh runs in memory of those she lost and to demonstrate resilience against cancer.

title: Markey presses Meta on facial recognition risks in smart glasses
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Senators Markey, Wyden, and Merkley question Meta's plans for facial recognition in smart glasses due to privacy concerns.
Lawmakers fear smart glasses could identify protesters and discourage public dissent, undermining privacy and civil liberties.
Meta has until April 6 to address concerns about data storage, opt-out options, and potential misuse of biometric data.

title: Debate in Quincy over funding for new firefighting gear after current gear tests positive for PFAS
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Quincy City Council delayed a vote on a $2.65 million plan to replace PFAS-contaminated firefighting gear.
PFAS, linked to health risks, were found in current gear, prompting city officials to seek new equipment.
Councilors postponed the decision due to concerns about cost and durability, frustrating local firefighters and unions.

title: The battle over the aging Attleboro Zoo is getting wild
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Attleboro proposes transforming Capron Park Zoo into a nature reserve and wildlife rehabilitation center to cut costs.
The zoo requires $57 million in repairs, prompting discussions on its future amidst declining attendance and rising expenses.
Zoo officials emphasize stability during the proposal phase, urging residents to participate in the discussion.

title: What does it mean to have a partner?
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Watertown newlyweds Abigail Brown and Colin Wong adapted to a blizzard by planning a last-minute microwedding in Somerville.
A new TV show 'Love Story' on FX revisits John F. Kennedy Jr. and Carolyn Bessette's relationship, highlighting 1990s communication.
Meredith Goldstein's Love Letters explores the evolving use of the term 'partner' in relationships and its potential implications.

title: After loss and cancer, a Burlington runner keeps moving toward Boston
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Adam Averback, from Burlington, MA, runs the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team MGH after facing personal losses and health challenges.
After his father's death from glioblastoma and surviving testicular cancer himself, Averback finds purpose in running marathons.
A nearly derailing blood infection tested Averback's resolve, but he persevered, training to honor his family's journey.

title: New Patriots S Kevin Byard working out with rumored New England trade target A.J. Brown
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Patriots showed strong interest in WR A.J. Brown; Eagles declined New England's trade offer.
Philadelphia may trade Brown post-June 1 to save cap space; seeking a first-round pick.
Brown worked out with Patriots' Kevin Byard, fueling speculation about potential trade.

title: James Hagens watch begins as Bruins brace for top prospect’s potential arrival
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: Boston College forward James Hagens concluded his sophomore season with 47 points in 34 games.
Bruins are eyeing Hagens' potential NHL debut after BC's season-ending loss in the Hockey East semifinals.
James Hagens, a 2025 seventh overall pick, may join Bruins soon, adding depth to their playoff-bound roster.

title: 5 must-watch movies & TV shows streaming right now
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Bradley Cooper directs Will Arnett in 'Is This Thing On?' available on Disney+, about a comedian coping with divorce.
Chuck Norris's 'Lone Wolf McQuade' streams on Tubi, Pluto TV, Plex, Prime Video, and YouTube following his death at age 86.
'Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man' continues Tommy Shelby's story, streaming on Netflix, set in 1940 Birmingham during WWII.
